input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode once to install any additional components
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release — check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui)

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release
- The installer includes the `dotnet` CLI
- **Verify:** `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 3. MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui`

## 4. Android SDK + JDK (handled automatically)

The MAUI workload installs the **Android SDK** and a compatible **JDK** (Microsoft OpenJDK) for you. If you need to manage them manually:

- **Android:** Android Command Line Tools from [developer.android.com/studio#command-tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools), or install Android Studio
- **JDK:** Microsoft OpenJDK from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload` pins compatible Android SDK / JDK versions. Run `dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n` on a project to see resolved paths.

## 5. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | Use VS Code instead |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

## 6. Verify everything end-to-end

```bash
# Check toolchain
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list
xcodebuild -version

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start one first via Android Studio or AVD)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

> Adjust `net9.0-*` to match your SDK version (`net8.0-*`, etc.).

## Key gotcha checklist

- **Rosetta 2** (Apple Silicon): Xcode handles this, but if you hit issues: `softwareupdate --install-rosetta`
- **iOS simulator runtime**: Xcode may prompt to download it separately (Settings → Platforms)
- **Android emulator on Apple Silicon**: Use an `arm64-v8a` system image, not x86
- **Provisioning (real iOS device)**: Requires an Apple Developer account and signing profile configured in your `.csproj`
